title: No Demand
definition: A narrow up bar on lower volume after weakness, showing lack of professional support for higher prices.
confirmation: Next bars fail to continue upward or close weakly.
commonMistake: Calling every low-volume up bar no demand without checking background.

title: No Supply
definition: A narrow down bar on lower volume after strength, showing lack of selling pressure.
confirmation: Next bars hold or move higher.
commonMistake: Ignoring whether the market has already shown strength.

title: Upthrust
definition: A move above resistance or prior high that closes weakly, often trapping breakout buyers.
confirmation: Failure to hold above the level and bearish follow-through.
commonMistake: Entering short before the bar closes.

title: Shakeout
definition: A push below support that quickly recovers, often testing and removing weak hands.
confirmation: Recovery above level and later no-supply/test behavior.
commonMistake: Buying every break below support without confirmation.

title: Stopping Volume
definition: High volume after a decline with reduced downside progress, suggesting absorption of selling.
confirmation: Market stops making meaningful lower closes and later tests supply.
commonMistake: Buying immediately into high volume without waiting.

title: Buying Climax
definition: Very high volume after an extended rise where price struggles to close strongly.
confirmation: Rally failure, upthrust, no demand, or break of local structure.
commonMistake: Assuming high volume always means strength.

title: Test
definition: A controlled move to check whether supply remains after prior strength.
commonMistake: Calling any small candle a test.

title: Effort With Poor Result
definition: High volume or wide spread produces little progress, showing opposition to the move.
confirmation: Next bars reveal who absorbed the effort.
